title: Flaky Fresh Argentinian Pasta Empanadas
description: A unique twist on traditional empanadas, featuring a gouda cheese filling wrapped in a flaky pasta dough and served with a side of salsa criolla.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and confectioners' sugar.
Add the cold butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg, pasta water, salt, and black pepper.
Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on how large you want your empanadas to be.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon or two of the gouda cheese in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the cheese to form a half-circle shape, and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the empanadas for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the empanadas hot with a side of salsa criolla.
